message:
test: agent/role coherence validation across pane theming and style system

Add 8 tests validating agent accent/bg/icon distinctness, role color
pairwise uniqueness, a11y marker disambiguation, and no-icons marker
suppression across all known agents and presets.

Closes: 2dccg.10.2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -482,7 +482,7 @@
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1.5","title":"Build ratatui→ftui no-feature-loss crosswalk and non-regression checklist","description":"## Goal
Create an explicit feature/functionality crosswalk so no user-critical capability is accidentally dropped while polishing FTUI visuals.

## Background
The current effort improves visual quality aggressively. Without a formal crosswalk, teams can unknowingly regress interaction affordances or information density while making the UI prettier.

## Work Items
- Inventory user-facing capabilities from ratatui v0.1.64 vs current FTUI:
  - search input cues and editing behavior
  - filter pills (state, affordance, click targets)
  - result list hierarchy (score/date/host/source/snippet)
  - detail tabs and active-state clarity
  - detail find bar + match counters + key flows
  - role differentiation and metadata readability
  - footer HUD/status/degradation diagnostics
  - keyboard + mouse navigation affordances
- Produce a must-not-regress checklist with explicit pass/fail criteria
- Map each checklist line item to owning beads/tests so coverage is auditable

## Deliverables
- Self-contained parity crosswalk artifact in bead comments/docs
- Non-regression checklist referenced by verification/release tasks

## Acceptance
- Every important legacy capability has an explicit FTUI status (kept/improved/replaced)
- Any intentional behavior change is documented with rationale
- Final release evidence includes checklist pass results","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:04:32.042455793Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:48:01.225926479Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T21:48:01.225905009Z","close_reason":"Completed crosswalk and non-regression checklist with ownership mapping for release verification","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:04:32.042455793Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:04:57.441442318Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:04:55.742649893Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:05:02.207975123Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":333,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:
1) Treat this bead title+description as the authoritative scope; do not rely on external planning docs.
2) Record touched files/functions and behavior surfaces in completion notes.
3) Provide deterministic before/after evidence (tests, snapshots, logs, or captures as applicable).
4) Validate across relevant themes/profiles/breakpoints/degradation levels for this surface.
5) Explicitly document tradeoffs, residual risks, and follow-up opportunities.
6) Do not close until acceptance criteria are demonstrably satisfied with linked evidence artifacts.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:17Z"},{"id":409,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Delivered self-contained ratatui->ftui crosswalk + non-regression checklist artifacts.\n\nTouched files:\n- docs/ftui_visual_parity_rubric.md\n- docs/ftui_visual_parity_manifest.json\n\nDeliverables completed:\n1) Capability crosswalk table covering all required user-facing surfaces with explicit status (kept/improved/replaced/at_risk)\n2) Non-regression checklist (NR-01..NR-08) with pass criteria, current state, and owning beads\n3) Machine-readable  +  sections in manifest for release-gate consumption\n4) Explicit gate policy identifying required-pass checks for install-script unpin\n\nEvidence anchors include deterministic tests/artifacts from src/ui/app.rs internal test matrix and the parity artifact bundle built under 2dccg.1.2.\n\nResidual risk accounting:\n- At-risk checks NR-02/NR-03/NR-05 are explicitly mapped to open beads (8.2/8.3/6.2, 9.1/9.2/9.3, 4.1/4.2/4.3).\n- No capability line-item remains unowned.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:47:38Z"},{"id":410,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Correction: previous comment had shell backtick interpolation side effects. Canonical deliverables are in docs/ftui_visual_parity_rubric.md and docs/ftui_visual_parity_manifest.json. Manifest includes explicit fields named crosswalk and non_regression_checklist consumed by release-gate tasks.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:47:49Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10","title":"Track J: Theme/env/capability hardening (overrides, adaptive policy, semantics)","description":"## Objective
Harden style-system correctness under real-world environment variability so visual quality is consistent across presets, terminal capabilities, and override settings.

## Why This Track Exists
Prior FTUI rehab backlog identified unresolved hardening work in palette semantics, env flag normalization, persisted override wiring, adaptive border/decor policy, and degradation transition quality.

## Scope
- Semantic palette + role tint correctness
- Env/capability behavior normalization (`NO_COLOR`, `CASS_*`, TERM profile)
- Persisted theme override runtime wiring
- Adaptive border/decor policy by capability + budget
- Smooth degradation transitions preserving hierarchy

## Deliverables
- Predictable styling behavior across environments
- Reduced “it looks wrong only on my terminal” incidents
- Stronger invariant tests around style-system behavior

## Completion Criteria
- Style-system behaves consistently across capability matrix
- Overrides and environment flags are tested and documented
- No hidden policy cliffs under runtime pressure","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:45:42.436018451Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:37.157311078Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:45:42.436018451Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":380,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Track orchestration note:
- This bead is a coordination container. Prefer executing ready leaf tasks under this track.
- Keep scope boundaries explicit to avoid duplicate implementation across sibling tracks.
- Ensure subtask closure writes enough evidence/context so future contributors can continue without external documents.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.1","title":"Reinforce semantic palette token source and style-system mapping correctness","description":"## Goal
Ensure semantic tokens resolve from a coherent palette model and remain stable across presets.

## Work Items
- Audit token derivation strategy in `style_system.rs`
- Remove ambiguous/duplicated token derivations
- Validate role/status/token contrast across presets

## Acceptance
- Token semantics are consistent and documented
- Palette changes propagate predictably to all surfaces
- No hidden regressions in token lookup behavior","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:47:01.213724961Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:45:12.553656385Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T21:45:12.553630647Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:47:01.213724961Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":334,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:
1) Treat this bead title+description as the authoritative scope; do not rely on external planning docs.
2) Record touched files/functions and behavior surfaces in completion notes.
3) Provide deterministic before/after evidence (tests, snapshots, logs, or captures as applicable).
4) Validate across relevant themes/profiles/breakpoints/degradation levels for this surface.
5) Explicitly document tradeoffs, residual risks, and follow-up opportunities.
6) Do not close until acceptance criteria are demonstrably satisfied with linked evidence artifacts.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:17Z"},{"id":408,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Palette token correctness — DONE. Fixed 3 upstream theme color collisions: (1) role_user changed from resolved.primary to blend(accent,success,0.35) to avoid primary==info on Light and accent==info on Dark, (2) score_low changed from text_subtle to blend(text_subtle,bg,0.35) to avoid info==text_subtle on Nord. Added 7 validation tests: role/gutter/status pairwise distinctness, text hierarchy ordering, score hierarchy, contrast report, deterministic propagation. All 40 style_system tests pass across all 6 presets.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:45:05Z"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.2","title":"Restore/validate agent-specific pane theming and role semantic color strategy","description":"## Goal
Preserve clear role/agent differentiation while maintaining cohesive overall theme.

## Work Items
- Audit agent accent style assignment and role-color interplay
- Ensure list/detail panes use role semantics consistently
- Validate accessibility fallback when color signal is reduced

## Acceptance
- Agent/role differences are visible and not confusing
- Theme coherence is maintained across all presets
- A11y markers complement color rather than replacing clarity","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:47:01.299478887Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:17.982712585Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:47:01.299478887Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:54:05.676134370Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":335,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:

Preserve clear role/agent differentiation while maintaining cohesive overall theme.

## Work Items
- Audit agent accent style assignment and role-color interplay
- Ensure list/detail panes use role semantics consistently
- Validate accessibility fallback when color signal is reduced

## Acceptance
- Agent/role differences are visible and not confusing
- Theme coherence is maintained across all presets
- A11y markers complement color rather than replacing clarity","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:47:01.299478887Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:19:39.720710511Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T22:19:39.720680725Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:47:01.299478887Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:54:05.676134370Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":335,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:
1) Treat this bead title+description as the authoritative scope; do not rely on external planning docs.
2) Record touched files/functions and behavior surfaces in completion notes.
3) Provide deterministic before/after evidence (tests, snapshots, logs, or captures as applicable).
4) Validate across relevant themes/profiles/breakpoints/degradation levels for this surface.
5) Explicitly document tradeoffs, residual risks, and follow-up opportunities.
6) Do not close until acceptance criteria are demonstrably satisfied with linked evidence artifacts.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:17Z"},{"id":430,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Agent/role coherence validation — DONE. Changes:

**theme.rs (5 tests):**
1. agent_accent_colors_are_pairwise_distinct — 10 agents, 45 pairs
2. agent_background_colors_are_pairwise_distinct — 10 agents (codex/pi_agent share intentionally)
3. agent_icons_are_pairwise_distinct — 10 agents
4. unknown_agent_falls_back_gracefully — fallback produces valid theme + icon
5. role_colors_are_pairwise_distinct_in_palette — dark palette user/agent/tool/system

**style_system.rs (3 tests):**
1. agent_accent_style_is_bold_for_all_agents — 11 agents (incl. unknown), all have fg + bold
2. role_markers_provide_text_disambiguation_in_a11y — markers non-empty, pairwise distinct
3. role_markers_empty_when_no_icons — no_icons suppresses markers

Architecture note: agent colors live in a separate parallel system (theme.rs PackedRgba constants) from style tokens (style_system.rs resolved theme). This is by design — agent identity is brand-level, not theme-adaptive. The two systems interact only at agent_accent_style() which wraps the PackedRgba in an ftui Style.

Totals: 69 style_system tests + 51 theme tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:18:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.3","title":"Add style-system invariant tests for profiles, overrides, and semantic guarantees","description":"## Goal
Provide foundational unit-level assurance for **style-system core semantics** (profile downgrade, override precedence, and semantic token contracts).

## Scope Boundary (Canonical)
This bead owns the **core style-system contract layer** consumed by multiple tracks.
- Includes: `style_system.rs` invariants for profile downgrade behavior, environment/override precedence, and semantic token guarantee checks.
- Excludes: cross-surface rendering matrix and interaction/event routing; those belong to `2dccg.11.1` and `2dccg.11.2`.

## Work Items
- Add tests for profile downgrade behavior (`mono/ansi16/ansi256/truecolor`) and monotonic style loss.
- Add override merge + precedence tests (theme config vs env flags vs runtime options).
- Add semantic token presence/contrast guarantees for critical tokens used by pills/tabs/gutters/find bar.
- Emit invariant-oriented failure messages that identify preset/profile/token and violated contract.

## Acceptance
- Core style-system invariants are enforced by automated tests.
- Failures clearly identify violated invariant and execution context.
- Provides reusable guarantees that downstream rendering/E2E suites can build on without duplicating precedence logic.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:47:02.023999373Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:08:47.873466620Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T22:08:47.873433278Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:47:02.023999373Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:54:02.816457171Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:53:59.204999477Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:54:01.719381666Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":336,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:
1) Treat this bead title+description as the authoritative scope; do not rely on external planning docs.
2) Record touched files/functions and behavior surfaces in completion notes.
3) Provide deterministic before/after evidence (tests, snapshots, logs, or captures as applicable).
4) Validate across relevant themes/profiles/breakpoints/degradation levels for this surface.
5) Explicitly document tradeoffs, residual risks, and follow-up opportunities.
6) Do not close until acceptance criteria are demonstrably satisfied with linked evidence artifacts.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:18Z"},{"id":414,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Canonical boundary update: this bead now explicitly owns core style-system contract tests (profiles/overrides/token guarantees), while rendering-surface matrix coverage lives in 11.1.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:33Z"},{"id":428,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Style-system invariant tests — DONE. Added 7 invariant tests:
1. profile_downgrade_is_monotonic — verifies Color fidelity (Rgb→Ansi256→Ansi16→Mono) decreases monotonically across all presets
2. override_partial_merge_preserves_unset_slots — overriding text doesn't change background
3. config_base_preset_overrides_options_preset — ThemeConfig.base_preset wins over StyleOptions.preset
4. critical_fg_tokens_always_have_foreground — 16 tokens × 6 presets × 3 profiles = 288 assertions
5. critical_bg_tokens_always_have_background — 4 tokens × 6 presets × 3 profiles = 72 assertions
6. a11y_mode_adds_emphasis_to_roles — roles resolve with fg in a11y mode
7. gutter_tokens_derive_from_role_tokens — GUTTER.fg==ROLE.fg and GUTTER has bg

Total: 66 style_system tests pass. No code behavior changes — pure test coverage.","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:08:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.4","title":"Normalize env/capability behavior (NO_COLOR, CASS flags, TERM/profile overrides)","description":"## Goal
Make runtime style behavior deterministic under environment flags and terminal capability variance.

## Work Items
- Define precedence rules for color/style env flags
- Validate capability detection and override handling
- Add tests for edge cases where flags conflict

## Acceptance
- Env-driven behavior matches documented policy
- No inconsistent styling between equivalent env setups
- Troubleshooting becomes straightforward with explicit rules","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:47:02.064211709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:50:38.100541249Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T21:50:38.100519708Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:47:02.064211709Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:54:03.407243187Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":337,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:
1) Treat this bead title+description as the authoritative scope; do not rely on external planning docs.
2) Record touched files/functions and behavior surfaces in completion notes.
3) Provide deterministic before/after evidence (tests, snapshots, logs, or captures as applicable).
4) Validate across relevant themes/profiles/breakpoints/degradation levels for this surface.
5) Explicitly document tradeoffs, residual risks, and follow-up opportunities.
6) Do not close until acceptance criteria are demonstrably satisfied with linked evidence artifacts.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:18Z"},{"id":418,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Env/capability normalization — DONE. (1) Added formal precedence doc comment to from_env_values() with 4-level priority table and cascade rules. (2) Added 12 edge-case tests: NO_COLOR without respect flag, falsy env_truthy values (0/false/off/no/FALSE/Off), invalid CASS_COLOR_PROFILE fallback, a11y→no_gradient cascade, no_icons independence from color state, dark_mode per preset, unknown theme fallback, gradients_enabled logic, env_truthy edge cases, full precedence matrix, mono profile smoke test, all presets×all profiles exhaustive smoke test. Total: 52 style_system tests pass. No code behavior changes — only documentation and test coverage.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:50:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.5","title":"Wire persisted theme overrides into normal runtime style resolution","description":"## Goal
Ensure theme editor/persisted overrides are fully respected by runtime rendering.

## Work Items
- Verify override load/merge flow in startup/runtime path
- Ensure all render paths consume resolved override-aware styles
- Add tests for override persistence and invalid override fallback

## Acceptance
- Persisted overrides are visible immediately and consistently
- Invalid overrides fail safely with clear diagnostics
- No path bypasses override-aware style context","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:47:02.127638201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:03:59.637649342Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T22:03:59.637628583Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:47:02.127638201Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:53:58.035503479Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":338,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:
1) Treat this bead title+description as the authoritative scope; do not rely on external planning docs.
2) Record touched files/functions and behavior surfaces in completion notes.
3) Provide deterministic before/after evidence (tests, snapshots, logs, or captures as applicable).
4) Validate across relevant themes/profiles/breakpoints/degradation levels for this surface.
5) Explicitly document tradeoffs, residual risks, and follow-up opportunities.
6) Do not close until acceptance criteria are demonstrably satisfied with linked evidence artifacts.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:19Z"},{"id":419,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Theme override wiring — DONE. Changes:

**app.rs:**
1. Added `theme_config: Option<ThemeConfig>` field to CassApp struct
2. Startup loading: `Default::default()` now loads `data_dir/theme.json` (if present), applies base_preset/dark_mode to initial options (behind `#[cfg(not(test))]`)
3. `resolved_style_context()` now uses `from_options_with_theme_config()` when `theme_config` is Some, with fallback to `from_options()` on invalid config
4. `ThemeEditorExported` handler now stores `theme_config = Some(config)` and updates `theme_dark`/`dark_mode` to match

**style_system.rs:**
Added 7 override wiring tests: override_applies_color_to_resolved_theme, override_base_preset_switches_dark_mode, override_empty_colors_does_not_change_theme, override_invalid_version_is_rejected, override_invalid_color_is_rejected, override_with_downgrade_still_works, override_fallback_on_invalid_returns_base_context.

Total: 59 style_system tests pass. Clippy clean, fmt clean.","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:03:53Z"}]}
@@ -514,7 +514,7 @@
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.3","title":"Add render/interaction tests for detail find bar visual states","description":"## Goal
Ensure find bar polish remains stable under future refactors.

## Scope Boundary (Canonical)
This bead owns **detail-find-bar component-level render/interaction regression checks**.
- Includes: visible find-bar states and local find-bar interaction outcomes in the detail pane.
- Excludes: global event-routing/state-machine ownership (`2dccg.11.2`) and broader cross-surface rendering matrix ownership (`2dccg.11.1`).

## Work Items
- Add tests for closed/open/no-matches/has-matches/current-match states.
- Add snapshot or line-structure assertions for styled find bar output.
- Verify `Esc` closes find bar without closing detail modal.
- Add focused assertions for match-counter formatting consistency across presets.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Existing behavior tests pass and new visual-state tests catch regressions.
- Match counter behavior remains correct after styling changes.
- Suite stays narrowly scoped to detail find bar outcomes (no cross-surface duplication).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:20.043890205Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:05:14.327208462Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:20.043890205Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:39:36.251889021Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":357,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","title":"Track E: Recalibrate degradation policy to preserve essential styling","description":"## Objective
Tune degradation behavior so terminals with sufficient capability keep meaningful styling rather than prematurely collapsing into bland output.

## Why This Exists
A suspected contributor to the quality drop is over-aggressive degradation logic tied to frame budget/terminal capability that strips colors/backgrounds too early.

## Scope
- Audit degradation decisions in render pipeline
- Define minimum visual hierarchy that should survive moderate degradation
- Adjust thresholds/policy and validate against representative terminal profiles

## Deliverables
- Documented degradation policy table (capability -> retained style layers)
- Updated heuristics/thresholds or guards in code
- Regression checks for no-style / low-style / full-style scenarios

## Completion Criteria
- Capable terminals retain tabs/pills/role hierarchy consistently
- Low-capability terminals remain readable without broken contrast
- Behavior is deterministic and testable (not ad-hoc per frame)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:47.279389078Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:28.544781336Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:47.279389078Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:38:30.698611175Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":253,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Track E Intent
FrankenTUI’s degradation system is powerful, but if tuned too aggressively it can erase the very hierarchy we worked to build.

### Strategy
Audit first, then tune. Avoid blind threshold changes. Preserve readability and hierarchy while keeping low-capability terminals functional.","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:41:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.1","title":"Audit degradation decision flow and identify style layers stripped per level","description":"## Goal
Make degradation behavior explicit and inspectable.

## Work Items
- Trace degradation inputs (terminal capabilities, frame budget, runtime heuristics)
- Enumerate which style layers are disabled at each degradation stage
- Produce a compact matrix: degradation level -> retained UI affordances

## Deliverables
- Auditable report attached to bead comments/docs
- Candidate list of over-aggressive strips affecting pills/tabs/gutters/markdown

## Acceptance
- Team can point to exact logic responsible for visual flattening
- Findings are specific enough to guide policy adjustments in follow-up tasks","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:29.152141848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:10:16.179984071Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T22:10:16.179961128Z","close_reason":"Completed degradation-flow audit with code-path mapping, per-level strip matrix, and follow-up candidate list documented in bead comments.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:56:39.253370420Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:29.152141848Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":358,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:
1) Treat this bead title+description as the authoritative scope; do not rely on external planning docs.
2) Record touched files/functions and behavior surfaces in completion notes.
3) Provide deterministic before/after evidence (tests, snapshots, logs, or captures as applicable).
4) Validate across relevant themes/profiles/breakpoints/degradation levels for this surface.
5) Explicitly document tradeoffs, residual risks, and follow-up opportunities.
6) Do not close until acceptance criteria are demonstrably satisfied with linked evidence artifacts.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:26Z"},{"id":429,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Audit findings (2026-02-08) — degradation decision flow and stripped-style matrix

Code-path map
1. Runtime budget profile is configured in `src/ui/app.rs:12753-12760` using `FrameBudgetConfig { total: 120ms, allow_frame_skip: false, degradation_cooldown: 12, upgrade_threshold: 0.25, ..relaxed() }`.
2. Render-time degradation gates are read in `src/ui/app.rs:11615-11637` (`frame.degradation`) and projected into four booleans: `use_unicode_borders()`, `render_decorative()`, `apply_styling()`, `render_content()`.
3. Those gates directly control:
   - border type fallback (`Rounded` -> `Square`) at `src/ui/app.rs:11619-11623`
   - whether pane/modal borders render (`Borders::ALL` vs `Borders::NONE`) at `src/ui/app.rs:11626-11630`
   - whether semantic styles are applied vs plain monochrome at `src/ui/app.rs:11636-11647` and related style slots
   - whether search query inset styling is shown at `src/ui/app.rs:11804-11815`
   - whether pane content body is rendered (analytics/sources/modal content paths) at `src/ui/app.rs:12138-12358`
4. Degradation-level semantics come from ftui in `ftui-render/src/budget.rs:621-730`:
   - `Full`: unicode borders + styling + decorative + content
   - `SimpleBorders`: unicode off, styling/decor/content still on
   - `NoStyling`: unicode off, styling off, decor/content on
   - `EssentialOnly`: unicode off, styling off, decorative off, content on
   - `Skeleton`: content off (layout shells only)
   - `SkipFrame`: max degradation

Observed stripping matrix (current behavior)
- `Full`: no strip
- `SimpleBorders`: rounded/box-drawing affordance downgraded to square/ascii-like look
- `NoStyling`: all semantic color/attribute hierarchy collapses to plain style (chips/tabs/gutters cues flatten)
- `EssentialOnly`: borders/decorative chrome removed (`Borders::NONE`) while content remains
- `Skeleton`: content text suppressed in gated paths; pane shells persist
- `SkipFrame`: max level; in this app frame-skip is disabled (`allow_frame_skip=false`), but visual output remains at effectively skeleton/no-content behavior where gates apply

Cross-check with tests
- Enum progression + gate semantics validated in `src/ui/app.rs:16452-16479`.
- Border suppression expectation validated in `src/ui/app.rs:17129-17152`.

Candidate over-aggressive flattening contributors for follow-up (`2dccg.5.2` / `2dccg.10.6`)
- Hard style collapse at `NoStyling` removes semantic differentiation too early for capable terminals under transient pressure.
- `render_decorative()` gate currently disables all borders at `EssentialOnly`, which can reduce scannability in dense panes.
- Decision thresholds for controller/e-process are largely inherited from ftui defaults (`ftui-render/src/budget.rs:342-357`, `454-481`) and should be revalidated against cass workload characteristics.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:08:48Z"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.2","title":"Tune degradation thresholds and smooth transitions for capable terminals","description":"## Goal
Rebalance degradation so essential hierarchy survives on capable terminals, and avoid abrupt visual cliffs when degradation level changes at runtime.

## Scope Boundary (Canonical)
This bead owns **runtime degradation transition dynamics**.
- Includes: threshold tuning, hysteresis/debouncing, and smooth level-to-level transition behavior.
- Excludes: capability-to-feature policy mapping for decorative/border strategy (`2dccg.10.6`), which consumes these tuned transition dynamics.

## Phase 1: Threshold Tuning (original 2dccg.5.2)
- Review current degradation trigger points (frame time, memory, resize frequency).
- Identify which style layers are most impactful for readability vs purely decorative.
- Raise thresholds where terminals can handle richer rendering without instability.
- Validate that tuned thresholds don't cause flickering or oscillation.

## Phase 2: Smooth Transitions (absorbed from 2dccg.10.8)
- Add intermediate states between degradation levels to prevent jarring visual jumps.
- Implement hysteresis or debouncing so level changes settle before committing.
- Ensure style layers fade/simplify gradually rather than vanishing all at once.
- Preserve key hierarchy markers (bold headers, role gutters, active-tab indicators) across transitions.
- Test transition smoothness under rapid resize and theme-switch scenarios.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Capable terminals preserve full-fidelity rendering under normal workload.
- Degradation transitions are smooth enough that users don't notice level changes.
- No oscillation between levels under stable conditions.
- Key information hierarchy survives at every degradation level.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:37.221785028Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:05:20.451224870Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:57:44.059146259Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:37.221785028Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:39:45.759251758Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":359,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.3","title":"Add degradation and capability-matrix regression tests with diagnostic fixtures","description":"## Goal
Guard against future over-degradation and provide strong observability for style-policy decisions across terminal profiles.

## Scope Boundary (Canonical)
This bead owns **policy-level degradation and capability-matrix regression coverage**.
- Includes: degradation-level retention/stripping invariants, capability profile expectation tests, policy diagnostics.
- Excludes: cross-surface visual matrix aggregation (`2dccg.6.3`) and full user-journey playback ownership (`2dccg.11.3`/`2dccg.11.7`).

## Phase 1: Degradation-Mode Regression Tests (original 2dccg.5.3)
- Create test fixtures for each DegradationLevel (Full, SimpleBorders, NoStyling, EssentialOnly).
- Test that key UI affordances (pills, tabs, role gutters, find bar) remain functional at every level.
- Verify that information hierarchy (headers > body > metadata) survives degradation.
- Assert specific style properties preserved/stripped at each level boundary.
- Test interaction behavior (keyboard nav, mouse clicks) is unaffected by degradation.

## Phase 2: Capability-Matrix Diagnostics (absorbed from 2dccg.10.9)
- Define capability matrix: color profile x Unicode support x frame budget x terminal type.
- Implement diagnostic output showing which style policies are active for a given capability set.
- Create test fixtures for common terminal profiles (xterm-256color, screen, dumb, Windows Terminal, kitty).
- Add tests that verify style-policy decisions match documented intent for each profile.
- Provide machine-readable diagnostic output for debugging unexpected visual behavior.
- Validate that NO_COLOR, CASS_RESPECT_NO_COLOR, and TERM override behavior is consistent.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A regression that over-strips styles at any degradation level is caught by tests.
- Each capability profile has documented expected behavior and tested invariants.
- Diagnostic output enables fast triage of 'why does it look wrong on terminal X?' reports.
- Tests remain fast enough for regular local runs (< 5s for full matrix).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:44.553982074Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:05:25.822343510Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:58:26.534372521Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:58:23.248572736Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:44.553982074Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:39:46.292623674Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":360,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:

Explicit include/exclude contracts now separate component-level, surface-level, policy-matrix, and cross-cutting verification responsibilities so parallel execution is safer and less duplicative.","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:04:10Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.4","title":"Expose degradation state in diagnostics/status for easier field triage","description":"## Goal
Make active degradation state visible so bug reports can include concrete runtime context.

## Work Items
- Ensure status/footer diagnostics clearly show current degradation mode
- Add optional debug detail for why a mode was selected (capability vs perf)
- Document how to capture this in issue reports

## Acceptance
- Agents can quickly determine whether visual flatness is due to degradation mode
- Troubleshooting no longer requires deep code spelunking for basic state info","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:52.039824793Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:42:01.574187601Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T20:42:01.574155942Z","close_reason":"Superseded and merged into canonical diagnostics bead 2dccg.10.9 to avoid overlap while preserving scope.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:52.039824793Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:39:46.478017233Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6","title":"Track F: Rich fixtures + snapshot coverage for visual regression protection","description":"## Objective
Upgrade test fixtures and snapshot coverage so the UI quality we care about is actually exercised by automated tests.

## Why This Exists
Current snapshots use minimal synthetic content that under-represents real-world visual complexity, allowing regressions in styling, hierarchy, and affordances to slip through.

## Scope
- Create richer fixture data (multi-agent roles, tool calls, markdown/code blocks, long snippets, score spread)
- Add snapshot variants covering pills, tabs, detail view, and role gutters
- Ensure tests are deterministic and cheap enough for regular local/CI runs

## Deliverables
- Expanded fixture builders/data helpers
- New snapshot baselines for key visual surfaces
- Test docs clarifying how to regenerate snapshots when intentional UI changes happen

## Completion Criteria
- Visual regressions in key surfaces produce obvious snapshot diffs
- Fixtures represent realistic cass workloads (not toy text-only lines)
- Snapshot updates are deliberate and reviewable","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:56.403007457Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:28.796092040Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:56.403007457Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:38:30.594645265Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":254,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Track F Intent
If tests do not model realistic UI complexity, regressions will keep recurring.

### Testing Philosophy
Prefer smaller targeted snapshots over giant omnibus snapshots. Keep fixtures rich, deterministic, and purpose-specific so diffs are explainable.","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:41:40Z"}]}
